reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 2 (Trust Boundary) and Gate 3 (Code Verification) triggered. The attack scenario requires loading a malicious ChainSpec config with max_blobs_per_block>4096. This is a local configuration file under operator control (semi-trusted boundary per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json trust_assumptions.local_validator). Configuration errors are explicitly out-of-scope. Additionally, Phase 03's claim that 'blocks with 4500 commitments pass runtime check at line 885' is factually incorrect: blob_kzg_commitments is typed as VariableList<KzgCommitment, U4096> (consensus/types/src/beacon_block_body.rs:14-15), and SSZ deserialization enforces this 4096 limit before any block reaches the runtime check at block_verification.rs:885. A block with >4096 commitments fails SSZ decode and never reaches validation. The spec requirement from 01e_PARTIAL_W0B1_1771748647.json line 431-446 ('All BLOB_SCHEDULE max_blobs_per_block values must be <= MAX_BLOB_COMMITMENTS_PER_BLOCK') is a configuration validation requirement, not a runtime exploit vector. Requires compromised local config file, outside security model.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: All BLOB_SCHEDULE max_blobs_per_block values must be <= MAX_BLOB_COMMITMENTS_PER_BLOCK. (01e_PARTIAL_W0B1_1771748647.json)
